criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies cherry-picking trade allocations as a distinct allegation category with its own dedicated section titled 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations.' It describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP (68.4% profitable allocation rate vs. 41.2% for other accounts) and Chao's personal account (68.6% profitability rate in November 2021 vs. 45.2% overall), along with specific financial figures ($7,800,000 in excess profits for Oceanic Fund I LP and $438,000 for Chao's personal account).

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not cite Sections 206(1) or 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 in connection with the cherry-picking allegation. The cherry-picking section discusses the scheme and financial harm but references no statutory provisions. Other sections cite Rule 206(4)-7, Rule 204-2, Section 207, and Section 209(e), but neither Section 206(1) nor Section 206(2) appears anywhere in the output in relation to the cherry-picking allegation.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. The Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations section explicitly states 'the evidence clearly indicates that WCA engaged in a pattern of cherry-picking trades allocated to specific client accounts and personal accounts' and references WCA throughout the analysis, including its compliance failures, trading volumes, and the financial harm caused to WCA's other advisory clients.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Whitmore Identified as Implicated (12% LP Interest)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies Whitmore's 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP and connects it to the cherry-picking allegation. Specifically, the output states: 'Whitmore holds a 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP, representing approximately $22.2 million in value as of December 31, 2023... This personal investment, combined with the management fees and performance-based compensation earned by WCA (through Whitmore GP Holdings LLC) as general partner, creates a significant financial interest for Whitmore in the performance of Oceanic Fund I LP.' This directly implicates Whitmore in the cherry-picking scheme through his financial interest in the favored account.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Chao Identified as Implicated (Personal Account)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies Chao as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation due to her personal account receiving favorable allocations. The output explicitly states that 'Hargrove-Tilton is alleged to have generated an additional $438,000 in excess profits by cherry-picking trades for a personal account of Chao' and references OQR analysis showing 'Chao's personal account received a higher rate of profitable allocations compared to other accounts' with specific profitability rates (68.6% vs 45.2% overall). The quantitative data table also shows Chao's personal account with a separate t-statistic of 5.21 (p < 0.001), further confirming her identification as implicated.
